title: lupinus argenteus subsp. spathulatus
intro: Lupinus argenteus subsp. spathulatus, also known as the silvery lupine, is a subspecies of the lupine plant that is native to the western United States. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lupinus argenteus subsp. spathulatus include reducing symptoms of menopause and menstrual cramps, as well as providing antioxidant and antidiabetic properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support women's health and alleviate symptoms of PMS.
